raw.title: Masonry — Headed Anchor Bolt (Shear Connection)
raw.body_text: Project 💾 Save📂 Load 🖨 Summary📄 Full Calc⊞ Expand All⊝ Collapse 🖨 Print / PDF Masonry — Headed Anchor Bolt (Shear Connection) Strength Design per TMS 402-22 — Tension, Shear & Interaction (MDG 2022 REK-08 SD) TMS 402-22 SD §9.1.6.3 Anchor Bolts Eq. 9-1 through 9-7 §6.3 Bolt Geometry MDG 2022 REK-08 SD Wall face Apt = πlb² lb thead do lbe → see Plan Grout ≥2 courses baf bvf Elevation Bolt geometry & Apt tension cone Near Edge of Masonry bvf (shear) lbe Apv = π lbe²/2 Plan View Apv shear half-cone (looking down) 1 — Masonry Properties Masonry Compressive Strength, f'm (psi) Nominal Wall Thickness (in) — context only 4 in. nominal (3.63 in. actual) 6 in. nominal (5.63 in. actual) 8 in. nominal (7.63 in. actual) 10 in. nominal (9.63 in. actual) 12 in. nominal (11.63 in. actual) 16 in. nominal (15.63 in. actual) 2 — Anchor Bolt Geometry TMS 402-22 §6.3 Nominal Bolt Diameter, do (in) 3/8 in. 1/2 in. 5/8 in. 3/4 in. 7/8 in. 1 in. 1-1/8 in. 1-1/4 in. Threads per Inch, nt 32 TPI (3/8") 24 TPI (7/16") 20 TPI (1/2") 18 TPI (1/2" fine) 16 TPI (5/8") 14 TPI (3/4" fine) 10 TPI (3/4" — A36/F1554) 9 TPI (7/8") 8 TPI (1" and larger) 7 TPI (1-1/8") 0 — use gross area (threads outside shear plane) Bolt Area, Ab (in²) — auto Per TMS 402-22 Eq. 6-7: Ab = π/4 × (do − 0.9743/nt)² Specified Embedment (in) Measured from face of masonry to end of bolt Bolt Head Thickness, thead (in) Typical headed bolt: 0.5 in. Effective Embedment, lb (in) — auto lb = Specified embedment − thead Edge Distance (shear dir.), lbe (in) From edge of masonry to bolt center, in direction of shear. Use grouted zone limit if applicable. Bolt Spacing, s (in) Center-to-center. Used to check Apt overlap (overlap if s < 2lb). 3 — Bolt Material Properties Bolt Grade / Specification ASTM A36 / F1554 Gr.36 — fy=36 ksi, fu=58 ksi ASTM F1554 Gr.55 — fy=55 ksi, fu=75 ksi ASTM A193 B7 — fy=105 ksi, fu=125 ksi ASTM A307 / F1554 Gr.105 — fy=92 ksi, fu=120 ksi Custom fy (psi) fu (psi) fu,eff = min(fu, 1.9fy, 125,000 psi) 4 — Factored Loads per Bolt Strength Design — LRFD Both demands are factored (ultimate) loads per bolt. baf = factored tension (pull-out); bvf = factored resultant shear. For the ledger angle shear connection in REK-08, these are derived from load combinations applied to the bolt group. Factored Bolt Tension, baf (lb) Tension pulling bolt out of masonry (from prying / out-of-plane loads) Factored Bolt Shear, bvf (lb) Resultant shear = √(Vy² + Vz²) for 3-D connections 5 — Shear Failure Mode Applicability Check which failure modes apply. Masonry crushing always applies. Breakout (Eq. 9-4) applies only when bolt is near a free edge in the shear direction. Pryout (Eq. 9-6) applies when bolt is deeply embedded and away from an edge (not a through-bolt). Eq. 9-4 Masonry Breakout (near edge in shear direction) Eq. 9-5 Masonry Crushing (always applies) Eq. 9-6 Pryout (deeply embedded, not a through-bolt) Eq. 9-7 Steel Shear (always applies) ▶ Calculate Anchor Bolt Capacity ✓ 0.50"-∅ bolt PASSES — IR = 0.000 ≤ 1.0 | φBan = 5,728 lb | φBvn = 2,039 lb Governing modes: Tension — steel | Shear — Masonry Breakout (Eq.9-4) TENSION CAPACITY — TBOLT = BAF VS. ΦBAN Masonry Breakout 6,092 lb φBanb (Eq.9-1, φ=0.50) Steel Tensile ▶ GOVERNS 5,728 lb φBans (Eq.9-2, φ=0.75) SHEAR CAPACITY — VBOLT = BVF VS. ΦBVN Masonry Breakout (Eq.9-4) ▶ GOVERNS 2,039 lb φ=0.5, nom=4,079 lb Masonry Crushing (Eq.9-5) 3,495 lb φ=0.5, nom=6,990 lb Pryout (Eq.9-6) 12,184 lb φ=0.5, nom=24,368 lb Steel Shear (Eq.9-7) 2,978 lb φ=0.65, nom=4,582 lb 5,728 lb φBan — Tension Capacity 2,039 lb φBvn — Shear Capacity 0.000 baf / Ban demand ratio 0.000 bvf / Bvn demand ratio 0.000 Interaction Ratio (≤ 1.0 required) Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Embedment Length lb lb = emb−thead; min = max(4do,2") TMS 402 §6.3.4/6.3.6 4.66 in. 2.00 in. min 232.8% OK ▶ Calc Bolt Tension baf vs. φBan steel governs (φ=0.50 breakout / 0.75 steel) TMS 402 §9.1.6.3.1 0 lb 5,728 lb 0.0% OK ▶ Calc Bolt Shear bvf vs. φBvn Masonry Breakout (Eq.9-4) governs TMS 402 §9.1.6.3.2 0 lb 2,039 lb 0.0% OK ▶ Calc Interaction (tension + shear) (baf/Ban)^5/3 + (bvf/Bvn)^5/3 TMS 402 §9.1.6.3.3 0.000 ≤ 1.0 0.0% OK ▶ Calc COMPUTED BOLT PROPERTIES Property Value Bolt diameter do 0.50 in. Threads per inch nt 10 TPI Bolt area Ab 0.127 in.² (π/4 × (0.500−0.9743/10)² = π/4 × 0.403²) Specified embedment 5.00 in. Head thickness thead 0.34 in. Effective embedment lb 4.66 in. Min. lb (4do or 2") 2.00 in. → OK Projected tension area Apt 68.11 in.² = π×4.66² Edge distance (shear) lbe 3.81 in. Projected shear area Apv 22.80 in.² = π×3.81²/2 fu,eff = min(fu, 1.9fy, 125k) 60,000 psi φBan (tension capacity) 5,728 lb φBvn (shear capacity) 2,039 lb Interaction ratio IR 0.000 ✓ OK Engineering Notes: This calculator implements Strength Design (SD) per TMS 402-22 Chapter 9 for headed anchor bolts. For Allowable Stress Design (ASD), use TMS 402-22 Chapter 8 (Eq. 8-1 through 8-7, with Bvc = 580 × ⁴√(f’m × Ab)). Masonry crushing (Eq. 9-5): Bvnc = 1750 × (f’m × Ab)1/4. This is the 4th root of (f’m × Ab). If bolt spacing s < 2lb = 9.31 in., the projected tension areas overlap and Apt must be reduced per TMS 402-22 §6.3.2. Currently the full Apt is used — verify manually. Edge distance lbe for shear should reflect the effective grouted zone limit when only partial grouting is present (not the full edge distance to the parapet top). ASCE 7-22 Section 14.4.5.1 requires increasing factored design forces by 2.0 when a non-ductile (masonry-controlled) failure mode governs in SDC D or higher. This calculator does not automatically apply this amplification — verify with the project seismic design category. This calculator does not design the steel ledger angle, welds, or joist seat. See MDG REK-08 SD for full shear connection design discussion. ⇄ Results Hub ⇄ Results Hub×
